steps.title: Find the center of symmetry
steps.content:
Absolute value equations $|f(x)| = k$ have solutions symmetric around where $f(x) = 0$. Solving $4x - 8 = 0$:
$$4x = 8$$
$$x = 2$$
The two solutions are equidistant from $x = 2$.

steps.title: Calculate the sum using symmetry
steps.content: When two values are equidistant from a center point, their average equals that center. Since the center is $x = 2$, the average of the two solutions is 2, so their sum is $2 \times 2 = 4$.
